channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Union Minister Requests Telangana to Sign PPA with NTPC to Meet Rising Electricity Demand
item.description: Union Minister for Coal and Mines, G Kishan Reddy, has called upon the Telangana government to sign a power purchase agreement (PPA) with the National Thermal Power Corporation (NTPC) to address the s...

item.title: EACOP launches programme to support girls’ school attendance with menstrual hygiene
item.description: DAR ES SALAAM: The East African Crude Oil Pipeline (EACOP) project imeanzisha programme mpya ya shule inayoitwa “Keep a Girl in School” chini ya Social Economic Investment and Corporate Social Respons...

item.title: Kenya Engages TikTok to Boost Digital Safety Before Elections
item.description: Nairobi — Kenya is stepping up efforts to make TikTok safer as the country prepares for the next election cycle. CS William Kabogo of the Information, Communications and Digital Economy Ministry has s...

item.title: Plaintiffs’ lawyer supports $30M settlement for former students abused at Vancouver-area Catholic schools
item.description: A $30-million settlement has been proposed for over 200 former students who allege sexual, physical, and psychological abuse at two Vancouver-area Catholic boys' schools, St. Thomas More Collegiate an...

item.title: Martin Lewis criticises social media over deepfake scam targeting his wife
item.description: Martin Lewis, the prominent UK financial broadcaster, has publicly expressed his frustration and lack of trust in social media platforms following the circulation of a deepfake video featuring his wif...

item.title: Fundraising Event in Regina Supports Local Hospitals and Autism Services
item.description: A recent fundraising event in Regina, titled 'An Evening in Greece,' successfully raised thousands of dollars to benefit local hospitals and autism support programs. The event featured cultural activi...

item.title: Authorities Investigate Security Lapses After Parañaque Condo Shooting
item.description: Authorities in Parañaque are conducting an investigation into security lapses at a condominium following a shooting incident that occurred on March 4, 2026. The suspect, William de Guzman, had been ev...

item.title: Taxi Drivers in Nyanga Protest for Improved Police Response and Safety
item.description: On Monday morning, commuters in Nyanga and neighbouring areas were left stranded as taxi operators suspended services over safety concerns following recent violent incidents at the Nyanga taxi rank. L...

item.title: Tinubu Assures Nigerians of Economic Recovery and Defeat of Terrorists at Interfaith Iftar Event
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu hosted traditional rulers and religious leaders from across Nigeria's six geo-political zones for an interfaith breaking of fast at the Presidential Villa in Abuja on March...

item.title: Scientists uncover mechanism behind rare blood clots linked to adenovirus-based COVID vaccines
item.description: COVID-19 vaccines, particularly those using modified adenoviruses such as the AstraZeneca vaccine, saved millions of lives during the pandemic. However, a rare adverse effect emerged shortly after rol...

item.title: Nearly Half of California Teachers Plan to Retire or Leave Profession in Next Decade, Survey Finds
item.description: A recent Education Week survey reveals that between 40% and 49% of California public school teachers (estimated at 45%) intend to retire or quit within the next 10 years, a higher rate than the nation...

item.title: Nigeria Moves Closer to Establishing State Police to Improve Security
item.description: Since 2014, the idea of establishing state police for Nigeria don dey for discussion, especially as the centralized Nigeria Police Force never fit handle the growing security challenges for the countr...

item.title: Floods Cut Off Cefane Hoek Residents Amid Years of Neglected Infrastructure
item.description: Residents of Cefane Hoek, a small rural village near Ngcobo in the Eastern Cape, are facing serious access problems after heavy rains destroyed the only bridge linking the community to neighbouring to...

item.title: Implications of Mojtaba Khamenei's Appointment for US-Iran Relations
item.description: Mojtaba Khamenei has been appointed as Iran's new supreme leader, succeeding his father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. This transition occurs amid ongoing attacks by US and Israeli forces, raising concerns ...

item.title: Lagos Government Announces Carter Bridge Reconstruction and Lists Alternative Routes for Commuters

item.title: UK MPs Reject Immediate Ban on Social Media Use for Under-16s, Consultation Planned
item.description: UK Members of Parliament voted against an amendment that would have banned children under 16 from using social media. The amendment was proposed by the Conservative Party as part of the Children's Wel...

item.title: Feeld App Expands Beyond Kink Community, Facing Mixed Reactions from Users
item.description: Feeld, a dating app originally launched in 2014 as 3nder, was created to cater to people with nontraditional sexual preferences and alternative relationship styles, including kink and polyamory. Over ...

item.title: Kenya's national reform agenda at a critical crossroads
item.description: The article analyses how Kenya's broader reform agenda, often referred to as the 'reform pact', is now at a decisive point where implementation will determine whether the country genuinely moves towar...

item.title: Enhancing Urban Management for Nairobi's Sustainable Growth
item.description: Nairobi faces big challenges from rapid growth and unplanned development, making effective urban management and planning very urgent. The article points out issues like traffic jams, poor waste manage...

item.title: UN Secretary-General Guterres expresses support for Philippines' bid for non-permanent UN Security Council seat during meeting with President Marcos
